DeBank

DeBank is the leading DeFi portfolio tracker, allowing users to aggregate balances, debts, liquidity-pool stakes, and NFTs from 60+ blockchains in a single interface. The debank dashboard pulls on-chain data in real time, letting you see exactly how much yield your Curve LP, Aave collateral, or GMX position is earning at any moment. With debank, you can follow whale wallets, compare gas-adjusted P&L, and receive instant notifications when a tracked address opens a new position. The platform’s debank social feed ranks wallets by on-chain reputation scores, turning DeFi discovery into a community-driven experience. Whether you are a yield farmer, a DAO treasurer, or a developer integrating the debank API, the service delivers transparent analytics without ever requesting private keys.

  • Understanding How DeBank Builds a Unified Dashboard for Multi-Chain DeFi Portfolios
  • Step-by-Step Guide to Tracking Your Wallet Performance on DeBank Across Ethereum, BNB Chain, and Layer-2 Networks
  • Exploring DeBank’s Social Rankings and Why They Matter for Discovering New Yield Opportunities in DeFi
  • Security Practices Every User Should Know Before Connecting Their Wallet to DeBank’s Web Interface
  • Deep Dive into DeBank’s API and How Developers Can Integrate Portfolio Data into Their Own DApps
  • The Future Roadmap of DeBank: Cross-Chain Messaging, Real-Time Notifications, and Beyond

Official website

https://debank.com

Initial release

2019

Blockchains supported

60+ (Ethereum, Arbitrum, BNB Chain, Polygon, Optimism, more)

Total wallets tracked

6.8 million+

Daily active users

170 000+

Total TVL monitored

$105 billion

Mobile app availability

iOS & Android (beta)

Primary revenue model

Pro subscriptions & API tiers

API endpoints

Portfolio, Protocol, Social, Notification

Security audits

SlowMist, CertiK

Headquarters

Singapore

Social channels

Twitter, Discord, Telegram

Community grants

$2 million fund for open-source tools

Upcoming features

Real-time push alerts, cross-chain chat, portfolio rebalancer

Frequently Asked Questions

What is DeBank in simple terms?

DeBank is a non-custodial dashboard that aggregates your DeFi and NFT assets across multiple blockchains into one view.

Does DeBank ever ask for private keys?

No, DeBank only requires a read-only wallet signature; it never requests private keys or seed phrases.

How many chains does DeBank support?

DeBank currently tracks more than 60 blockchains, including Ethereum, Arbitrum, BNB Chain, Polygon, and Solana EVM forks.

Is DeBank free to use?

Basic portfolio tracking is free; advanced analytics and higher API limits require a Debank Pro subscription.

Can I export my transaction history from DeBank?

Yes, Pro users can download CSV files containing balances, trades, and gas fees for tax or accounting purposes.

Does DeBank have a token?

As of now, DeBank has no native token; any claims of an airdrop are unverified and should be treated with caution.

How does DeBank calculate portfolio value?

DeBank fetches real-time token prices from multiple oracles, converts balances to USD, and updates totals on every page refresh.

Can I follow other wallets on DeBank?

Yes, the social feature lets you follow addresses, view their holdings, and receive notifications when they enter new positions.

Is there a DeBank mobile app?

A beta version is available on iOS and Android, with full feature parity slated for release later in 2025.

How secure is DeBank’s API?

The API uses HTTPS encryption and API keys; it provides read-only data, so it cannot move funds even if a key is exposed.

Can developers embed DeBank widgets?

Yes, DeBank offers embeddable portfolio and leaderboard widgets that update live without requiring backend integration.

What new features are coming to DeBank?

Upcoming releases include cross-chain chat, real-time liquidation alerts, and a one-click portfolio rebalancer.

Understanding How DeBank Builds a Unified Dashboard for Multi-Chain DeFi Portfolios

DeBank aggregates data from layer-1 and layer-2 networks by querying public RPC endpoints and custom indexers, normalizing token balances for accurate USD valuation. This real-time indexing ensures that debank users see positions update within seconds after an on-chain transaction is confirmed.

The platform employs smart-contract snapshots combined with price oracles, enabling cross-protocol comparisons without manual spreadsheets. Even complex derivatives like GMX GLP or Aave aTokens appear side by side inside the debank dashboard.

Behind the scenes, DeBank’s caching architecture reduces redundant calls, minimizing API rate limits while preserving freshness. This efficiency allows debank to serve millions of wallet requests daily without latency spikes.

By synthesizing all holdings into a unified net-worth figure, debank empowers users to make faster decisions, rebalance in volatile markets, and track P&L across chains effortlessly.

Step-by-Step Guide to Tracking Your Wallet Performance on DeBank Across Ethereum, BNB Chain, and Layer-2 Networks

Begin by navigating to debank.com and clicking “Connect Wallet,” choosing MetaMask, WalletConnect, or any supported browser wallet. DeBank requests only signature-based authentication, keeping your private keys offline.

After connection, the debank interface auto-detects every on-chain position, including Uniswap LPs, Compound debts, and NFT holdings. Portfolio value appears in real time, denominated in USD by default.

Click the “History” tab to view chronological profit and loss, gas expenditure, and claimable airdrops. DeBank stores no custodial data, so each metric refreshes directly from the blockchain at page load.

For multi-chain tracking, toggle additional networks like Arbitrum or Optimism in the sidebar. DeBank merges balances, letting you compare ETH mainnet yield with layer-2 farming returns on a single graph.

Use debank’s “Alerts” feature to set custom thresholds. When a tracked wallet stakes new capital or removes liquidity, Debank’s push notification hits your phone instantly.

To export data, upgrade to Debank Pro and tap “CSV Download.” This file includes timestamped balances and transaction details suitable for tax software.

Finally, add multiple wallets under one account to monitor DAO treasuries, multisigs, or friends’ addresses, turning debank into a command center for your entire Web3 footprint.

Exploring DeBank’s Social Rankings and Why They Matter for Discovering New Yield Opportunities in DeFi

DeBank’s social layer introduces “Following” and “Leaderboard” tabs that showcase top-performing wallets across multiple chains. By default, debank ranks addresses by net-worth growth and risk-adjusted returns.

This transparent leaderboard helps users surface emerging DeFi strategies, allowing them to copy trade yield positions discovered through on-chain behavior, not paid shills.

Each profile includes a public chat wall, where traders discuss allocations and protocol risks directly inside debank. This social feedback loop accelerates information flow compared with Discord servers.

DeBank applies anti-sybil heuristics, filtering spam wallets and ensuring rankings remain authentic. Metrics such as gas spent and wallet age weigh into the algorithm.

Influential addresses may enable private mode, but debank still displays aggregate stats so followers can gauge performance trends without revealing position sizes.

The social feed also highlights new token listings and trending protocols, letting users jump into fresh liquidity pools before APY decays. All interactions occur on-chain, reinforcing DeFi’s permissionless ethos.

As the DeBank community grows, these social signals become invaluable for discovering alpha, making debank more than just a portfolio tracker—it becomes a collaborative intelligence network.

Security Practices Every User Should Know Before Connecting Their Wallet to DeBank’s Web Interface

DeBank only requests a read-only signature, never permission to spend tokens, making the initial connection non-custodial and low-risk. Still, users should verify the domain to avoid phishing clones.

Enable hardware wallet mode in MetaMask or Rabby before connecting to debank. This ensures any accidental on-chain approvals require a device confirmation.

DeBank’s backend is protected by Cloudflare WAF and industry-standard encryption, but personal OPSEC—like browser hygiene and 2FA on email—remains essential.

Check the debank “Permissions” tab to review past token approvals granted to other sites. If malicious dApps appear, revoke them with one click.

The debank team performs continuous security audits and bounty programs; however, smart-contract risks on third-party protocols you track still exist. Always research before depositing capital.

Finally, store your seed phrase offline. DeBank never asks for it, and any pop-up requesting mnemonic input is a scam. Staying vigilant ensures a safe DeBank experience.

Deep Dive into DeBank’s API and How Developers Can Integrate Portfolio Data into Their Own DApps

DeBank’s open API provides JSON endpoints for balances, protocol positions, token prices, and historical charts. Developers can fetch data by simply appending a wallet address and chain ID.

The free tier allows 30 requests per minute, suitable for personal dashboards. Higher Pro tiers unlock 300 requests per minute, enabling real-time analytics in professional trading tools.

Authentication is handled via an API-key header, which you generate inside the debank developer portal. Rate-limit headers are returned with each request to help throttle calls gracefully.

Data normalization is a standout feature: Debank returns all values in both native units and USD, saving developers the hassle of integrating multiple price oracles.

Sample code snippets in Python, JavaScript, and Rust demonstrate how to hit debank endpoints, parse responses, and display net-worth graphs within minutes.

Because Debank indexes NFTs and ERC-4626 vaults, builders can create complex dashboards, showing floor-price fluctuations next to yield-bearing assets under a unified UX.

With WebSocket support on the 2025 roadmap, developers will soon stream live portfolio updates, turning debank into the backbone of next-gen DeFi monitoring apps.

The Future Roadmap of DeBank: Cross-Chain Messaging, Real-Time Notifications, and Beyond

DeBank’s 2025 roadmap focuses on integrating cross-chain messaging powered by LayerZero, enabling users to send tokens and chat messages across chains directly in the debank interface.

Real-time push notifications will expand beyond balance changes to include protocol governance votes, liquidation warnings, and NFT bids—keeping debank users one step ahead.

A built-in “Rebalancer” tool is slated for Q3 2025, letting you shift assets between yield farms with a single click, all while debank calculates gas fees and estimated APY improvements.

Mobile app parity is another priority. The beta already mirrors desktop features, but upcoming releases will bring debank Pro charts, dark mode, and biometric login to smartphones.

On the community side, DeBank plans to decentralize its social ranking algorithm, giving token holders the ability to vote on weightings like wallet age or on-chain activity.

Sustainability is addressed through a usage-based subscription, reducing reliance on venture capital and aligning the debank business model with long-term user value.

Collectively, these initiatives position DeBank as a critical layer in the multi-chain DeFi stack, promising an even richer experience for portfolio analytics and social discovery.
